rghisThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:41:10 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cayuga Lodge papers
Cayuga Lodge (Cornell University)
37-4-824
4.3 cubic feet. 4.3 cubic feet.
1937-1997. Correspondence, photographs, printed materials, publications Include materials documenting the history of this men's cooperative residence (now co-ed, formerly Llenroc Lodge and Cayuga Student Resident Association) Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1 4 2 3 5 electronic records ======= 1 4 2 3 5 electronic records >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2097899
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
March 2018
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, March 2018
Date modified:
RMC Staff, March 2018
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Include house minutes of this men's cooperative residence (now co-ed, formerly Llenroc Lodge and Cayuga Student Resident Association), as well as minutes of its executive committee, board of directors, and board of trustees, and of the Inter-Cooperative Council; committee aned financial reports; and constitutions, by-laws, codes, rosters, and programs of the organization. Also, correspondence, mainly of lodge advisor Edward A. Ott, concerning election of officers, functions of the lodge, name changes, and other business; other correspondents include Robert B. Gervan, Hugh A. Moran, and Robert E. Treman. Also the files of William Ballagh which include correspondence and publications documenting the history of the group during the 1930s and 1940s. Also digital scans of the Ballagh material.
RELATED MATERIALS
See also the thesis Building history : how the Cornell family's brick carriage house became Cayuga Lodge by Rebecca Baker.
Cite As:
Cayuga Lodge papers, #37-4-824. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:
Access to Boxes 1-5 restricted to the permission of the President of Cayuga Lodge.
Ballagh, William Ott, Edward A. Gervan, Robert B. Moran, Hugh Anderson, 1881-1977. Treman, Robert E. (Robert Elias), 1888-1953. Llenroc Lodge (Cornell University) Cayuga Student Residence Association. Cornell University, (Date of meeting or treaty signing: 1940-1955..) Cornell University -- : Students. Cornell University -- : Student housing.
2097899 <<<<<<< HEAD William Ballagh Cornell Class of 1937 files
Scope and Contents
These files and inventory were compiled March 28, 2017 by Tom Ballagh, '67, BS, Agr., Resident Member 1963-66, Associate Member 1966-67, from materials provided by his father, Bill Ballagh, '37, BS, Agr., Resident Member 1934-37, President of Permanent Membership Committee, 1937-44; Dick Kline,'64, BS, Agr., '65, MBA, Resident Member 1960-64, son of Dick Kline, '39, BS, Agr., Resident Member 1935-39; and others.Digital scans of Ballagh materialCorrespondence regarding the transfer of documents to the Cornell University Archives3/201761Member photographs1930s62Ithaca Journal article Llenroc Lodge Example of Co-op Enterprise3/30/193862"Sing to Llenroc" Song Sheet by Delmar Cooper63Llenroc Brochure, Students Can Live Economically While At Cornell University193763Westminster Presbyterian Student Society Brochure13934-193563Dr. Ott personal letter to Bill Ballagh remarking on house maintenance projects7/20/193563Llenroc Lodge Association new member welcome letter8/8/193563Llenroc Lodge Bulletin No. 2 "Living at Llenroc LodgeFall 193763Llenroc Lodge Association letter of commendation to Bill Ballagh3/11/193764Resolution by Board of Directors expressing appreciation to Bill Ballagh for 1936-1937 service64Letter from Bill Ballagh to Llenroc thanking the Association for commendation3/28/193764Llenroc Social Committee letter to Bill Ballagh4/5/193764Llenroc Lodge Association Permanent Membership invitation to Bill Ballagh4/27/193764Bill Ballagh acceptance of Permanent Membership status5/1/193764Permanent Membership Certificates, Cayuga Student Residence Association and Llenroc Lodge Association for Bill Ballagh5/326/193764Llenroc Lodge Cost Records as of July 19377/193765Analysis of Purchase Price of Llenroc Lodge prepared by Bill Ballagh193765Llenroc Lodge Bulletin #1 prepared by Edward A. OttSummer 193765Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh regarding house inventory8/4/193765Letter from Allan Treman to Bill Ballagh regarding preparation of certificate of incorporation8/5/193765Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh regarding progress on certificate of incorporation8/17/193765Llenroc Lodge letter from Jim Woodbury to Bill Ballagh requesting for assistance on progress of transfer of property10/5/193765Letter from Allan Treman to Bill Ballagh on proposed Certificate of Incorporation for Llenroc Lodge Association Incorporated10/9/193765Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh regarding signing application for incorporation charter10/9/193765Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh on incorporation progress and operation of the house10/14/193765Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh regarding signing application for incorporation charter10/26/193765Letter from Richard Milk to Bill Ballagh on reservations of signing incorporation charter11/9/193765Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh regarding the review of the Constitution and By-Laws and update on progress of the operation of the Lodge11/16/193766Certification of Election of Permanent Membership Committee Officers for 1937-1938193766By-Laws of Llenroc Lodge Association, copy of Bill BallaghFall 193766Committee on Revision of Constitution Report by Dr. Ott shared with Ballagh and F. Rosevearundated66Proposed Revised Constitution for Llenroc Lodge Association with revision notes by Bill Ballaghundated66Llenroc Lodge Code BookFall 193766Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh announcing the incorporated association12/7/193766Llenroc Lodge Permanent Membership Bulletin No.1 announcing approval for the charter of incorporation by the State of New York2/193866Llenroc Lodge Permanent Membership Bulletin No. 2Spring 193867Llenroc Lodge Permanent Membership Bulletin No. 3 effective 3/29/1938 at a joint meeting of the Permanent Membership Committee and Resident Membership Committee3/31/193867Llenroc Lodge Permanent Membership Bulletin No. 4 announcing Annual Meeting 6/11/1938undated67Letter from Dick Milk to Bill Ballagh explaining the purpose of the Annual Meeting on 6/11/1938 to officially sign the papers for the transfer of the house to the cooperative association3/24/193867Program brochure for Banquet and Joint Meeting3/29/193867Letter from Summer School Committee to Permanent Members updating progress of Lodge4/14/193867Llenroc Lodge Summer School Application193867Personal letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh regarding upcoming Joint Meeting in June and progress on house maintenance and membership, dated only "Monday"5/193868Llenroc Lodge News Letter, Vol. II No. 16/11/193868Letter from Richard Milk to Bill Ballagh requesting to be relieved of Secretary duties8/21/193868Llenroc Lodge News Letter, Vol II No. 2Fall 193868Llenroc Lodge News Letter, Vol II No. 3Winter 193868Letter from Jim Woodbury to Bill Ballagh on improvements in the house and request for a meeting of the Permanent Membership Committee12/9/193868Partial letter to Bill Ballagh listing names of Permanent Members2/1/193968Notice of Annual Meeting of Board of Trustees6/17/193968Llenroc Lodge News Letter Vol. II No. 45/16/193968Letter from Dick Milk to Bill Ballagh on need for a report to the Permanent Members5/17/193968Letter from Bill Ballagh to Ed Hume asking him to present letter to the Permanent Members6/16/193968Letter from Bill Ballagh to Permanent Members on the unique structure of Llenroc Lodge, duties of the Permanent Members and importance to the residents while living at the Lodge6/17/193969Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh advising of Llenroc Lodge extension to an empty house on Stewart Ave. with Ed Hume as the advisor. Discussed updates to the Lodge8/26/193969Letter from Allan Treman to Bill Ballagh on the issuance of a new bond for the mortgage9/27/193969Letter from Rubin Close to Bill Ballagh discussing the new mortgage9/28/193969Letter from Bill Ballagh to Rubin Close acknowledging receipt of above letters10/3/193969Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh regarding name change to Cayuga Lodge AssociationFall 1939610Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol. III No. 1 refers to Llenroc Lodge Annex at 614 Stewart Ave.10/6/1939610Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh regarding Delta Phi protesting Llenroc name4/23/1940610Letter from Bill Ballagh to L.N. Simmons requesting legal advice on Llenroc name5/2/1940610Letter from Bill Ballagh to Hugh Moran requesting University advice on name change5/3/1940610Letter from Bill Ballagh to Dr. Ott discussing name change5/3/1940610Letter from Hugh Moran to Bill Ballagh recommending Cayuga Lodge name with justification5/7/1940610Letter from Allan Treman to Bill Ballagh providing legal advice on name change5/11/1940610Cover letter from Bill Ballagh to Dr. Ott enclosing form letter to Permanent Members5/11/1940610Form letter from Bill Ballagh to Permanent Members discussing name change based on University and legal advice and requesting input from the members5/11/1940610Letter from Bill Ballagh to Hugh Moran referencing quoting his letter to Permanent Members5/11/1940610Postcard from G.E. Dunham to Bill Ballagh commenting on name change5/14/1940610Letter from Ralph Mungle to Bill Ballagh commenting on name change5/14/1940610Letter from L.N. Simmons to Bill Ballagh recommending "Cayuga Lodge"5/14/1940610Letter from Ed Wagoner to Bill Ballagh commenting on name change5/14/1940610Letter from Dan Kurshan, LLD to Bill Ballagh presenting legal advice on name change5/14/1940610Letter from Bob Foster to Bill Ballagh commenting on name change5/15/1940610Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol. III No. 25/18/1940610Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh discussing Resolution for Annual Meeting5/22/1940610Resolution for name change to Cayuga Lodge Association, Inc. for 5/20/1940 Annual Meeting5/1940610Form letter from Bill Ballagh to Permanent Members explaining Resolution on name change5/25/1940610Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh reporting on voting of Permanent Members on name change6/3/1940610Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh discussing problems with name change6/6/1940610Letter from William Burns to G.S. Tarbell discussing name change legal problems6/3/1940610Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh discussing Referendum ballot for Special Meeting on name change6/21/1940610Llenroc Lodge Special Meeting Referendum for 7/5/1940 Special Meeting for approving name change to Cayuga Student Residence Association, Inc.6/21/1940610Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh on change of street address to 630 Stewart Ave. and name change to Cayuga Student Residence Association, Inc.8/6/1940610Llenroc Lodge Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol. IV No. 1 announcing name change and other Lodge news6/25/1940610Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership, Board of Trustees through 1943, Permanent Membership Roster and mailing list of prospective members as of 6/30/19406/30/1940611Letter to Student Advisor from Membership Committee encouraging recruitment of new members, on Cayuga Student Residence Assn stationeryundated611Proposed letter to Bill Ballagh for review to all student residents on need to make payments on past due 1939-40 house bills, lists rental cost for each room. Funds needed for upkeep of building8/7/1940611Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh regarding his error on proposed letter to residents, receipt of funds from Delta Phi, new "630 Stewart Avenue" street sign8/12/1940611Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh on name and address change, new charter recorded on 7/18/19407/18/1940611Llenroc Lodge Assn. Board of Trustees Ballot list of 8 Nominees to elect 4 for 3-year terms 1940 – 1943611Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership Roster December 1940 & January 1941611Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh requesting review of proposed letter to repair driveway9/4/1940611Letter from Hugh Moran to Bill Ballagh on refinancing loan to Cayuga Lodge11/26/1940611Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol. IV No. 32/1/1941611
Scope and Contents
includes an open letter from Dr. Ott to Permanent Members on high academic rating of membersPersonal letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh commenting on problems at the Lodge, draft causing high member turn-over, discipline cases and new initiations5/6/1941612Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol III (sic.) No. 45/19/1941612Letter from Paul Fox to Bill Ballagh offering assistance for Annual Meeting on 6/14/19416/1941612Letter from Bill Ballagh to Paul Fox responding to Paul's suggestions6/12/1941612Receipt to Bill Ballagh for $5 for subscriptions to Business Week7/16/1941612Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh discussing payment problems for repointing brickwork, minutes of the Annual Meeting of 6/14/1941, attached recommending maintenance7/23/1941612Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol V No. 18/10/1941612Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh, Richard Haynes and Ed Wagner regarding maintenance needs of the house8/14/1941612Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership Roster as of 8/15/19418/15/1941612Tentative Statement to Permanent Members on the purpose of Cayuga Student Residence Associationundated612Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill and Jane Ballagh congratulating them on the birth (11/16/1941) of Donald William Ballagh11/28/1941612
Scope and Contents
(Note: Dr. Ott wrote similar congratulatory letters to former residents)Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol V No. 21/1/1942612Personal letter from Bruce Cormack from "Somewhere in Bataan"3/6/1942612
Scope and Contents
(Note: Bruce was later to survive the Bataan Death March and return home). Letter copied on the back is from Bruce's mother to Amy Ballagh (sister of Bill). Original of the letter was sent to Bruce in 1987 (see correspondence from Bruce Cormack to Tom Ballagh, '67 in Folder 16 section of the indexLetter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh regarding financial/employee problems in the Dining Room5/6/1942612Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh reporting on the election of officers5/29/1942612Form letter from Dr. Ott to residents of Cayuga Student Residence Assn. on the benefits of the Lodge and the need for continued recruiting of new members7/14/1942613Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh on results of the election of officers and open positions7/15/1942613Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh with brief update on house activities and request to discuss objectives for a permanent program to develop a practical plan for members9/4/1942613Report from Special Committee regarding ownership of Dining Association1942613Resolution on Cayuga Dining Association2/18/1943613Cayuga Student Residence Assn. listing officers through 1945 with vacancies for 1942-1943613Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol. V No. IV7/16/1942613Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh discussing vacancies in officers on Permanent Membership10/10/42613Letter from Bill Ballagh to Dr. Ott appointing Hartley Martin as Secretary-Treasurer of Permanent Membership Committee10/19/1942613Letter from Bill Ballagh to Hartley Martin appointing him as Secretary Treasurer10/19/1942613Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh with follow-up on officer vacancies10-19-1942613Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh requesting appointment of Roland Brill as Vice President11/23/1942613Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh with copies to Mr. Moran and Mr. Tompkins discussing loss of residents to the Draft and the possibility that the University may take over the Lodge to house military trainees attending Cornell. Proposed Amendment to the Constitution and changes in officers12/14/1942613Letter from Bill Ballagh to Dr. Ott responding to the 12/14/1942 letter12/19/1942613Cayuga Student Residence Assn. list of officers through 1945 and mailing list of Permanent Members (Note the number of Permanent Members listed as in Active {Military} Service)1942-1943614Announcement of Special Meeting of the Board of Trustees 2/25/1943 to consider Resolution on Cayuga Dining Assn signed by Dr. Ott2/19/1943614Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership Addresses as of 5/12/19435/1943614Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol. V No. 49/21/1943614Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol. VI No. 15/27/1944614
Scope and Contents
The stress of the War is evident in Dr. Ott's reporting. He expressed his appreciation for the condolences received for the death of his sister, Mrs. Hinshaw on January 2, 1944.Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol. VI No. 28/25/1944614Form letter by Dr. Ott entitled "NEVER STOP COOPERATING" encouraging recruitment of residents and benefits of cooperative living over fraternities1944614Minutes of Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Board of Trustees. Dr. Ott submitted his resignation as Resident Advisor which was accepted with regret and many expressions of appreciation6/18/1945614Circular letter from Dr. Ott on his declining eyesight and installation of the "Talking Book Machine" in his library. He requested letters from friends6/1945614From letter from Hugh Moran to Fellow Members of Cayuga Student Residence Assn. announcing the retirement of Dr. Ott and appointment of officers of the Board of Trustees under the war-time powers of the Association Constitution amendment dated 12/22/1942, letter dated 6/22/194519421945614Form letter from Hugh Moran to "All the Old Crowd at Cayuga Lodge"8/30/1945614
Scope and Contents
Announces Dr. Ott had moved from the Lodge and in with his daughter and two grandchildren on their farm. Mr. Moran was very hopeful for a prosperous future for the Lodge. He attached Lee Whitford's August 8, 1945 extensive report to the Permanent Members.Packet sent to early members of Llenroc Lodge12/1/1987615
Scope and Contents
sent to Lee Whitford and returned later by his widow, Jean Whitford following his deathLetter from Tom Ballagh, '67, BS requesting any information they may have had during the formative years of Llenroc Lodge especially Dr. Ott, copies of documents and pictures12/1/1987615Listing of names and addresses of known living Permanent Members from the 1930's615Letter from Bill Ballagh to Tom Ballagh on his memories of Llenroc Lodge11/9/1987615Resident Candidate Orientation Program on the history of Cayuga Lodge1963615Llenroc Brochure, "Students Can Live Economically While At Cornell University"1937615Llenroc Lodge Bulletin No. 2 "Living at Llenroc Lodge"Fall 1937615Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh regarding house inventory8/4/1937615Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh regarding progress on certificate of incorporation8/17/1937615Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh on incorporation progress and operation of the house10/14/1937615Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh regarding the review of the Constitution and By-Laws and update on progress of the operation of the Lodge11/16/1937615Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh announcing the incorporated association12/7/1937615Llenroc Lodge Permanent Membership Bulletin No. 3 effective 3/29/1938 at a joint meeting of the Permanent Membership Committee and Resident Membership Committee3/31/1938615Personal letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh regarding upcoming Joint Meeting in June and progress on house maintenance and membership, dated only "Monday"5/1938615Letter from Bill Ballagh to Permanent Members on the unique structure of Llenroc Lodge, duties of the Permanent Members and importance to the residents while living at the Lodge6/17/1939615Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh regarding Delta Phi protesting Llenroc name4/23/1940615Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol. III No. 25/18/1940615Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh discussing problems with name change6/6/1940615Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh regarding his error on proposed letter to residents, receipt of funds from Delta Phi, new "630 Stewart Avenue" street sign8/12/1940615Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol. IV No. 32/1/1941615
Scope and Contents
Includes an open letter from Dr. Ott to Permanent Members on high academic rating of resident membersLetter from Dr. Ott to Bill and Jane Ballagh congratulating them on the birth (11/16/1941) of Donald William Ballagh11/28/1941615Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh regarding financial/employee problems in the Dining Room5/6/1942615Form letter from Dr. Ott to residents of Cayuga Student Residence Assn. on the benefits of the Lodge and the need for continued recruiting of new members7/14/1942615Circular letter from Dr. Ott on his declining eyesight and installation of the "Talking Book Machine" in his library. He requested letters from friends, undated6/1945615From letter from Hugh Moran to Fellow Members of Cayuga Student Residence Assn. announcing the retirement of Dr. Ott and appointment of officers of the Board of Trustees under the war-time powers of the Association Constitution amendment19421945615Form letter from Hugh Moran dated 8/30/1945 to "All the Old Crowd at Cayuga Lodge" announcing Dr. Ott had moved from the Lodge and in with his daughter and two grandchildren on their farm. Mr. Moran was very hopeful for a prosperous future for the Lodge. He attached Lee Whitford's extensive report to the Permanent Members8/8/1945615Member Group Photo signed by members on back in position on photo1936-1937615Form letter from Tom Ballagh to Permanent Members requesting information, list of Permanent Members names and addresses for distribution is attached12/1/1987616William Ballagh Memoir11/19/1987616Letter from Mrs. F.B. Rosevear2/1/1988616Letter from Bill Oden2/3/1988616Postcard from Bob Nagel acknowledging receipt of Llenroc Lodge packet616Letter from Bob Nagel1/7/1988616Letter from Jess Young12/12/1987616Letter from Russ Martin12/17/1987616
Scope and Contents
(A quote from his letter: "My last recollection of Dr. Ott came in the 1950's. I knew that he was very ill, and while presiding at a meeting of the Board of Directors at the Lodge, we received word that he had passed away. It was a sad moment for all of us.")Letter from Art Schuyler1/3/1988616Letter from Dan Kurshan12/30/1987616Letter from Bruce Cormack12/13/1987616Letter from Roy E. "Jim" Woodbury12/4/1987616Note from Dick Kline, '6412/19/1987616Letter from Dick Kline, '6412/19/1987616Letter from Leigh Whitford12/13/1987616Letter from Francis T. Jones2/2/1988616
Scope and Contents
With attachments of original request and copy of Who's Who in Mineral Names ======= William Ballagh Cornell Class of 1937 files
Scope and Contents
These files and inventory were compiled March 28, 2017 by Tom Ballagh, '67, BS, Agr., Resident Member 1963-66, Associate Member 1966-67, from materials provided by his father, Bill Ballagh, '37, BS, Agr., Resident Member 1934-37, President of Permanent Membership Committee, 1937-44; Dick Kline,'64, BS, Agr., '65, MBA, Resident Member 1960-64, son of Dick Kline, '39, BS, Agr., Resident Member 1935-39; and others.Digital scans of Ballagh materialCorrespondence regarding the transfer of documents to the Cornell University Archives3/201761Member photographs1930s62Ithaca Journal article Llenroc Lodge Example of Co-op Enterprise3/30/193862"Sing to Llenroc" Song Sheet by Delmar Cooper63Llenroc Brochure, Students Can Live Economically While At Cornell University193763Westminster Presbyterian Student Society Brochure13934-193563Dr. Ott personal letter to Bill Ballagh remarking on house maintenance projects7/20/193563Llenroc Lodge Association new member welcome letter8/8/193563Llenroc Lodge Bulletin No. 2 "Living at Llenroc LodgeFall 193763Llenroc Lodge Association letter of commendation to Bill Ballagh3/11/193764Resolution by Board of Directors expressing appreciation to Bill Ballagh for 1936-1937 service64Letter from Bill Ballagh to Llenroc thanking the Association for commendation3/28/193764Llenroc Social Committee letter to Bill Ballagh4/5/193764Llenroc Lodge Association Permanent Membership invitation to Bill Ballagh4/27/193764Bill Ballagh acceptance of Permanent Membership status5/1/193764Permanent Membership Certificates, Cayuga Student Residence Association and Llenroc Lodge Association for Bill Ballagh5/326/193764Llenroc Lodge Cost Records as of July 19377/193765Analysis of Purchase Price of Llenroc Lodge prepared by Bill Ballagh193765Llenroc Lodge Bulletin #1 prepared by Edward A. Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol. IV No. 32/1/1941611
Scope and Contents
includes an open letter from Dr. Ott to Permanent Members on high academic rating of membersPersonal letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh commenting on problems at the Lodge, draft causing high member turn-over, discipline cases and new initiations5/6/1941612Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol III (sic.) No. 45/19/1941612Letter from Paul Fox to Bill Ballagh offering assistance for Annual Meeting on 6/14/19416/1941612Letter from Bill Ballagh to Paul Fox responding to Paul's suggestions6/12/1941612Receipt to Bill Ballagh for $5 for subscriptions to Business Week7/16/1941612Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh discussing payment problems for repointing brickwork, minutes of the Annual Meeting of 6/14/1941, attached recommending maintenance7/23/1941612Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol V No. 18/10/1941612Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh, Richard Haynes and Ed Wagner regarding maintenance needs of the house8/14/1941612Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership Roster as of 8/15/19418/15/1941612Tentative Statement to Permanent Members on the purpose of Cayuga Student Residence Associationundated612Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill and Jane Ballagh congratulating them on the birth (11/16/1941) of Donald William Ballagh11/28/1941612
Scope and Contents
(Note: Dr. Ott wrote similar congratulatory letters to former residents)Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol V No. 21/1/1942612Personal letter from Bruce Cormack from "Somewhere in Bataan"3/6/1942612
Scope and Contents
(Note: Bruce was later to survive the Bataan Death March and return home). Letter copied on the back is from Bruce's mother to Amy Ballagh (sister of Bill). Original of the letter was sent to Bruce in 1987 (see correspondence from Bruce Cormack to Tom Ballagh, '67 in Folder 16 section of the indexLetter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh regarding financial/employee problems in the Dining Room5/6/1942612Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh reporting on the election of officers5/29/1942612Form letter from Dr. Ott to residents of Cayuga Student Residence Assn. on the benefits of the Lodge and the need for continued recruiting of new members7/14/1942613Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh on results of the election of officers and open positions7/15/1942613Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh with brief update on house activities and request to discuss objectives for a permanent program to develop a practical plan for members9/4/1942613Report from Special Committee regarding ownership of Dining Association1942613Resolution on Cayuga Dining Association2/18/1943613Cayuga Student Residence Assn. listing officers through 1945 with vacancies for 1942-1943613Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol. V No. IV7/16/1942613Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh discussing vacancies in officers on Permanent Membership10/10/42613Letter from Bill Ballagh to Dr. Ott appointing Hartley Martin as Secretary-Treasurer of Permanent Membership Committee10/19/1942613Letter from Bill Ballagh to Hartley Martin appointing him as Secretary Treasurer10/19/1942613Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh with follow-up on officer vacancies10-19-1942613Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh requesting appointment of Roland Brill as Vice President11/23/1942613Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh with copies to Mr. Moran and Mr. Tompkins discussing loss of residents to the Draft and the possibility that the University may take over the Lodge to house military trainees attending Cornell. Proposed Amendment to the Constitution and changes in officers12/14/1942613Letter from Bill Ballagh to Dr. Ott responding to the 12/14/1942 letter12/19/1942613Cayuga Student Residence Assn. list of officers through 1945 and mailing list of Permanent Members (Note the number of Permanent Members listed as in Active {Military} Service)1942-1943614Announcement of Special Meeting of the Board of Trustees 2/25/1943 to consider Resolution on Cayuga Dining Assn signed by Dr. Ott2/19/1943614Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership Addresses as of 5/12/19435/1943614Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol. V No. 49/21/1943614Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol. VI No. 15/27/1944614
Scope and Contents
The stress of the War is evident in Dr. Ott's reporting. He expressed his appreciation for the condolences received for the death of his sister, Mrs. Hinshaw on January 2, 1944.Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol. VI No. 28/25/1944614Form letter by Dr. Ott entitled "NEVER STOP COOPERATING" encouraging recruitment of residents and benefits of cooperative living over fraternities1944614Minutes of Cayuga Student Residence Assn. Board of Trustees. Dr. Ott submitted his resignation as Resident Advisor which was accepted with regret and many expressions of appreciation6/18/1945614Circular letter from Dr. Ott on his declining eyesight and installation of the "Talking Book Machine" in his library. He requested letters from friends6/1945614From letter from Hugh Moran to Fellow Members of Cayuga Student Residence Assn. announcing the retirement of Dr. Ott and appointment of officers of the Board of Trustees under the war-time powers of the Association Constitution amendment dated 12/22/1942, letter dated 6/22/194519421945614Form letter from Hugh Moran to "All the Old Crowd at Cayuga Lodge"8/30/1945614
Scope and Contents
Announces Dr. Ott had moved from the Lodge and in with his daughter and two grandchildren on their farm. Mr. Moran was very hopeful for a prosperous future for the Lodge. He attached Lee Whitford's August 8, 1945 extensive report to the Permanent Members.Packet sent to early members of Llenroc Lodge12/1/1987615
Scope and Contents
sent to Lee Whitford and returned later by his widow, Jean Whitford following his deathLetter from Tom Ballagh, '67, BS requesting any information they may have had during the formative years of Llenroc Lodge especially Dr. Ott, copies of documents and pictures12/1/1987615Listing of names and addresses of known living Permanent Members from the 1930's615Letter from Bill Ballagh to Tom Ballagh on his memories of Llenroc Lodge11/9/1987615Resident Candidate Orientation Program on the history of Cayuga Lodge1963615Llenroc Brochure, "Students Can Live Economically While At Cornell regarding the review of the Constitution and By-Laws and update on progress of the operation of the Lodge11/16/1937615Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh announcing the incorporated Bill Ballagh to Permanent Members on the unique structure of Llenroc Lodge, duties of the Permanent Members and importance to the residents while living at the Lodge6/17/1939615Letter from Dr. Ott to Permanent Membership News Bulletin Vol. IV No. 32/1/1941615
Scope and Contents
Includes an open letter from Dr. Ott to Permanent Members on high academic rating of resident membersLetter from Dr. Ott to Bill and Jane Ballagh congratulating them on the birth (11/16/1941) of Donald William Ballagh11/28/1941615Letter from Dr. Ott to Bill Ballagh regarding financial/employee problems in the Dining Room5/6/1942615Form letter from Dr. Ott to residents of Cayuga Student Residence Assn. on the benefits of the Lodge and the need for continued recruiting of new members7/14/1942615Circular letter from Dr. Ott on his declining eyesight and installation of the "Talking Book Machine" in his library. He requested letters from friends, undated6/1945615From letter from Hugh Moran to Fellow Members of Cayuga Student Residence Assn. announcing the retirement of Dr. Ott and appointment of officers of the Board of Trustees under the war-time powers of the Association Constitution amendment19421945615Form letter from Hugh Moran dated 8/30/1945 to "All the Old Crowd at Cayuga Lodge" announcing Dr. Ott had moved from the Lodge and in with his daughter and two grandchildren on their farm. Mr. Moran was very hopeful for a prosperous future for the Lodge. He attached Lee Whitford's extensive report to the Permanent Members8/8/1945615Member Group Photo signed by members on back in position on photo1936-1937615Form letter from Tom Ballagh to Permanent Members requesting information, list of Permanent Members names and addresses for distribution is attached12/1/1987616William Ballagh Memoir11/19/1987616Letter from Mrs. F.B. Rosevear2/1/1988616Letter from Bill Oden2/3/1988616Postcard from Bob Nagel acknowledging receipt of Llenroc Lodge packet616Letter from Bob Nagel1/7/1988616Letter from Jess Young12/12/1987616Letter from Russ Martin12/17/1987616
Scope and Contents
(A quote from his letter: "My last recollection of Dr. Ott came in the 1950's. I knew that he was very ill, and while presiding at a meeting of the Board of Directors at the Lodge, we received word that he had passed away. It was a sad moment for all of us.")Letter from Art Schuyler1/3/1988616Letter from Dan Kurshan12/30/1987616Letter from Bruce Cormack12/13/1987616Letter from Roy E. "Jim" Woodbury12/4/1987616Note from Dick Kline, '6412/19/1987616Letter from Dick Kline, '6412/19/1987616Letter from Leigh Whitford12/13/1987616Letter from Francis T. Jones2/2/1988616
